message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1, fixes applied vs main). Venv: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
The file /tmp/pass1_confirm.json is a JSON array of 22 pass-1 findings (each: issue, title, file, sev, problem, suggested_fix).
Constraints (NOT bugs): local-first/data-sovereign, no metered API, engine imports no adapter, lazy optional backends.

You are independent CONFIRMER #2 of 2 for finding INDEX 21 (0-based) in /tmp/pass1_confirm.json.
Read that item (its issue number, problem, suggested_fix, file). Then VERIFY THE APPLIED FIX on the current branch: read the changed source AND its regression test (git diff vs main, or just read the file + tests), and where useful run a PoC against the venv to confirm the original problem is actually resolved.
Return: appropriate = the fix correctly addresses the root cause in a sound way (not a hack/incomplete patch/over-claim); complete = the fix fully resolves the finding AND has a regression test that would catch a regression. Set either false if not, and say exactly what is missing. Echo the issue number in 'issue'.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll analyze finding INDEX 21 in the pass-1 findings file.

content.content:
{
  "issue": "122",
  "title": "run_study emits phase/reasoning spans but never starts tracing or stamps run_id baggage \u2014 traces cannot be correlated to the run or the experiment-tracker run_id",
  "file": "/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py",
  "sev": "LOW",
  "problem": "run_study is the documented end-to-end composed runner and it opens trace spans for every phase (tracer.span('phase:INGEST') etc.) and the reasoning seam emits LLM/AGENT spans underneath. But run_study never calls bootstrap_tracing() and never wraps execution in run_trace_context(run_id) \u2014 grep of pipeline.py shows no reference to either. run_trace_context (tracing.py:145-163) is what stamps the 'assay.run_id' baggage onto every span so traces are correlatable across stores; the orchestration graph runner (graph.py:104-108) does exactly this, but the primary pipeline does not. Consequence: spa",
  "suggested_fix": "In run_study, after obtaining run_id (or a synthesized correlation id when no tracker is supplied), call bootstrap_tracing() once and wrap the phase machine in `with run_trace_context(corr_id):` so every phase and reasoning span is stamped with the run id and lands at the local collector \u2014 mirroring orchestration.graph.run(). Use the experiment_run_id when present so Phoenix spans and the MLflow r"
}

===TRACING 140-170===
        fn()
    except Exception:
        pass


@contextmanager
def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None) -> Iterator[None]:
    """Stamp ``run_id`` onto every span emitted inside the block (cross-store correlation).

    A no-op when ``run_id`` is None or OpenTelemetry is absent.
    """
    if run_id is None:
        yield
        return
    try:
        from opentelemetry import baggage, context
    except ImportError:
        yield
        return
    token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("assay.run_id", run_id))
    try:
        yield
    finally:
        context.detach(token)


# OpenInference requires every span carry a span kind so Phoenix classifies it (CHAIN/LLM/
# AGENT/TOOL); a plain OTel span with no kind renders as UNKNOWN in the Phoenix UI. We set the
# raw semantic-convention attribute directly (no openinference import needed for a manual span).
_OPENINFERENCE_SPAN_KIND = "openinference.span.kind"

content.content:
52:def bootstrap_tracing() -> Any:
53-    """Idempotently wire the global OTel tracer provider to the local collector.
54-
55-    Returns the provider, or ``None`` if tracing is disabled, the extra is not installed, or
56-    setup fails — tracing must never be load-bearing, so this degrades gracefully.
57-    """
58-    global _provider
59-    if _disabled():
60-        return None
61-    # Lock the check-then-set so two threads can't both register the global provider.
62-    with _provider_lock:
63-        if _provider is not None:
64-            return _provider
65-        try:
66-            require_loopback_host(TRACING_HOST, what="tracing collector host")
67-            # Bound the OTLP export so a downed collector can never hang teardown (#O3).
68-            os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
69-            from phoenix.otel import register
70-
71-            provider = register(
72-                project_name=PROJECT_NAME,
73-                endpoint=tracing_endpoint(),
74-                protocol="http/protobuf",  # pin: endpoint is .../v1/traces — never flip to gRPC
75-                set_global_tracer_provider=True,
76-                batch=True,  # async BatchSpanProcessor — never block the run on export (#O2)
77-                verbose=False,  # no stdout banner from a library context

Uses ``durability="sync"``: a governance run must have its parked-at-gate checkpoint written
    *before* the operator is notified, so a crash in the (otherwise async) write window cannot
    lose the interrupt. (The LangGraph default is ``"async"``.)
    """
    if trace:
        bootstrap_tracing()
    config = _thread_config(run_id, recursion_limit)
    with run_trace_context(run_id):
        return graph.invoke(state, config=config, durability="sync")


def resume(
    graph: Any,
    *,
    run_id: str,
    gate_id: str,

Let me verify there's no test anywhere that asserts the baggage/run_trace_context behavior, and run a quick PoC to confirm the fix actually works at runtime.

content.text: There are unit tests for `run_trace_context` and `bootstrap_tracing` in isolation, but those test the tracing module directly — not that `run_study` actually calls them. Let me confirm whether any test verifies the pipeline-level integration (the actual #122 root cause: run_study not calling these). Let me run a PoC that monkeypatches both functions and runs run_study to confirm they're invoked with the right id.

content.content:
def test_tracing_endpoint_rejects_non_loopback(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr(tr, "TRACING_HOST", "10.0.0.5")
    with pytest.raises(NonLocalEndpointError):
        tracing_endpoint()


def test_run_trace_context_noop_without_run_id():
    with run_trace_context(None):
        pass  # must not raise


def test_run_trace_context_noop_without_otel():
    # opentelemetry absent -> the context manager is a safe no-op even with a run_id
    with run_trace_context("run-1"):
        pass


def test_otel_tracer_span_is_noop_without_otel():
    with OtelTracer().span("x", {"a": 1}):
        pass  # must not raise when opentelemetry is absent

import contextlib
import assay_engine.pipeline as pl
import tests.reference_adapter as ref
from assay_engine.contracts.modes import Mode

calls = {'bootstrap': 0, 'ctx_ids': []}
orig_boot = pl.bootstrap_tracing
def fake_boot():
    calls['bootstrap'] += 1
    return None\[REDACTED]
def fake_ctx(rid):
    calls['ctx_ids'].append(rid)
    yield
pl.bootstrap_tracing = fake_boot
pl.run_trace_context = fake_ctx

import tempfile, pathlib
d = pathlib.Path(tempfile.mkdtemp())
src = ref.write_source(d/'c.json')
# tracker present -> run_id flows as corr id
class T:
    def start_run(self, name, params): return 'run-XYZ'
    def log_metric(self,*a): pass
    def log_artifact(self,*a): pass
    def end_run(self, rid, status='FINISHED'): pass
res = pl.run_study(ref.make_plan(src, modes=ref.ALL), tracker=T())
print('bootstrap calls:', calls['bootstrap'])
print('ctx ids:', calls['ctx_ids'])
print('expected corr == run_id from tracker (run-XYZ):', calls['ctx_ids'] == ['run-XYZ'])

content.text:
All 52 pass. Now I have a complete picture. Let me assess.

**Appropriateness:** The fix correctly addresses the root cause. It calls `bootstrap_tracing()` once, computes `corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex` (using the experiment-tracker run_id when present, synthesizing a uuid otherwise — exactly as the suggested fix prescribes), and wraps the entire phase machine in `run_trace_context(corr_id)` via an ExitStack that is closed in the `finally` block (so the baggage is correctly detached even on failure paths). This mirrors `orchestration.graph.run`. Both functions are best-effort no-ops when OTel/Phoenix is absent, preserving the lazy-optional-backend constraint. PoC confirms both branches (with/without tracker) behave as intended.

**Completeness — regression test:** This is where it falls short. The test diff for this batch added a regression test only for #109/#110 (`test_failed_run_records_failure_entry_and_marks_tracker_failed`) — the failure-recording and FAILED-status changes that ride along in the same diff. There is **no regression test asserting the #122 behavior**: that `run_study` actually invokes `bootstrap_tracing()` and enters `run_trace_context` with the correct correlation id (run_id when a tracker is present, a synthesized id otherwise). 

The unit tests in `tests/test_observability.py` only test `bootstrap_tracing`/`run_trace_context` in isolation — they would NOT catch a regression where someone deletes those two calls from `pipeline.py` (the exact original bug). I verified this: I had to write my own monkeypatch PoC to confirm the wiring, because no committed test does so. A regression that reverts the pipeline wiring would pass the entire suite green.

input.reasoning:
APPROPRIATE: yes. The applied fix in src/assay_engine/pipeline.py (run_study) is a sound root-cause fix that mirrors orchestration.graph.run. It calls bootstrap_tracing() once, computes corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex (uses the experiment-tracker run_id when present, synthesizes a uuid otherwise — exactly as the suggested_fix prescribes), and wraps the whole phase machine in run_trace_context(corr_id) via an ExitStack closed in finally, so the assay.run_id baggage is stamped on every phase/reasoning span and correctly detached even on failure. Both helpers are best-effort no-ops when OTel/Phoenix is absent (respects the lazy-optional-backend constraint). I confirmed main had zero references to either function (original problem was real) and ran a monkeypatch PoC against the venv showing: with a tracker, bootstrap called once + context entered with run-XYZ; without a tracker, bootstrap called once + context entered with a 32-char synthesized uuid. Full pipeline+observability suite passes (52 passed, 2 skipped).

COMPLETE: no — missing regression test for THIS finding. The test diff vs main (tests/test_pipeline.py) added a regression test only for the co-shipped #109/#110 (run_failed provenance entry + FAILED tracker status: test_failed_run_records_failure_entry_and_marks_tracker_failed). No test asserts the #122 behavior — that run_study actually invokes bootstrap_tracing() and enters run_trace_context with the correct corr_id (run_id when a tracker exists, synthesized id otherwise). The tests in tests/test_observability.py exercise bootstrap_tracing/run_trace_context only in isolation; they would NOT catch the exact regression of deleting those two calls from pipeline.py — that revert would pass the whole suite green (I had to write my own monkeypatch PoC because no committed test covers it). Missing: a pipeline-level test that monkeypatches pipeline.bootstrap_tracing and pipeline.run_trace_context and asserts (a) bootstrap is called, (b) the context is entered with the tracker's run_id when a tracker is supplied, and (c) entered with a non-None synthesized id when no tracker is supplied.

content.text:
Confirmed and reported. Summary for finding #122:

- **appropriate = true** — the applied fix in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py` correctly wires `bootstrap_tracing()` + `run_trace_context(corr_id)` into `run_study`, using the experiment-tracker run_id when present and a synthesized uuid otherwise, with proper teardown via ExitStack in `finally`. PoC against the venv confirms both branches behave as designed.
- **complete = false** — no regression test covers the #122 behavior itself. The co-shipped test only covers #109/#110. A revert of the two pipeline tracing calls would pass the whole suite green.
